CapGainsLossTxInvstIncmGrp.PropertyDesc: DYNAMIC EQUITY MANAGERS: PORTFOLIO 1 OFFSHORE, LP

SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description1Txt: SUPPORT FAMILIES TO IMPROVE OUTCOMES FOR CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING THROUGH LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE THROUGH HEARING FIRST
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description2Txt: SUPPORT FOR STARTS HEAR PUBLIC AWARENESS CAMPAIGN ABOUT THE IMPORTANCE OF HEARING TO BRAIN DEVELOPMENT AND KNOWING THE STATUS OF A NEWBORN'S HEARING SCREENING

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: AG BELL ASSOCIATION FOR THE DEAF AND HARD OF HEARING
FOUNDATION FOR HEARING RESEARCH INC WEINGARTEN CHILDREN'S CENTER
MAYO CLINIC ROCHESTER DIVISION OF GASTROENTEROLOGY & HEPATOLOGY
THE MOOG CENTER FOR DEAF EDUCATION

ContributorBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: MILDRED L OBERKOTTER CHARITABLE REMAINDER UNITRUST

GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE MISSION OF THE OBERKOTTER FOUNDATION (WWW.OBERKOTTERFOUNDATION.ORG) IS TO HELP FAMILIES ENSURE THEIR CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING HAVE OPPORTUNITIES TO REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL THROUGH LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE (LSL).

FAMILIES' NEEDSEARLY LEARNING IS CRITICAL TO A CHILD'S LANGUAGE DEVELOPMENT AND LIFETIME SUCCESS, AND CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING MUST HAVE THE SAME OPPORTUNITIES AS THEIR HEARING PEERS TO CAPITALIZE ON THE CRITICAL EARLY PERIOD FOR BRAIN DEVELOPMENT. FAMILIES MUST HAVE ACCESS TO QUALITY PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY AND LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE INTERVENTION AS CLOSE TO THEIR BABY'S BIRTH OR ACQUIRED HEARING LOSS AS POSSIBLE. AND FAMILIES MUST BE EMPOWERED AND EQUIPPED TO EFFECTIVELY SUPPORT THEIR BABY'S LEARNING, LANGUAGE AND LITERACY DEVELOPMENT. IT IS A FUNDAMENTAL PRINCIPLE AT OBERKOTTER THAT PARENTS DECIDE THE LANGUAGE AND MODALITY FOR THEIR CHILD TO PURSUE. ALL OF OBERKOTTER'S ACTIVITIES AND INVESTMENTS ARE INTEGRATED AND COORDINATED IN FURTHERANCE OF OUR GOAL OF HELPING CARING ADULTS TO SUPPORT INFANTS AND TODDLERS WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING TO HAVE THE SAME LISTENING, SPOKEN LANGUAGE AND LITERACY OPPORTUNITIES AS THEIR HEARING PEERS. WE CONDUCT OUR WORK BY SUPPORTING PROGRAMS AND PROJECTS THAT RAISE AWARENESS AND INCREASE ACCESS TO EDUCATION, RESOURCES, HEARING TECHNOLOGY AND COORDINATED SERVICES. OUR WORKSINCE OUR FOUNDING, THE TRUSTEES OF THE OBERKOTTER FOUNDATION HAVE BEEN COMMITTED TO CARRYING OUT THE OBERKOTTER FAMILY'S VISION THAT CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING SHOULD HAVE ACCESS TO LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE SERVICES IN THEIR COMMUNITY. OVER OUR 39-YEAR HISTORY, WE HAVE PROVIDED OVER $500 MILLION TO ORGANIZATIONS SERVING FAMILIES. (CONTINUED)IN JUNE 2023, THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES ADOPTED THE 2023-2027 OBERKOTTER FOUNDATION STRATEGIC PLAN GROUNDED IN OUR MISSION TO HELP FAMILIES ENSURE THEIR CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING HAVE OPPORTUNITIES TO REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL THROUGH LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE. THE PLAN IS DRIVEN BY A SET OF VISIONARY GOALS AND BOLD ACTIONS TO GROW OUR IMPACT AND PAVE THE WAY FOR A MORE INCLUSIVE AND SUPPORTIVE FUTURE FOR CHILDREN AND THEIR FAMILIES: 1. ADVANCE SCIENCE AND INNOVATION TO IMPROVE AUDIOLOGICAL, LISTENING, AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE (LSL), AND LITERACY OUTCOMES FOR CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING. 2. RAISE AWARENESS BROADLY OF THE NEWBORN HEARING SCREENING, THE IMPORTANCE OF HEARING TO BRAIN DEVELOPMENT, AND THAT BABIES WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING ARE LEARNING TO LISTEN AND TALK. 3. INCREASE NATIONWIDE ACCESS TO HEARING TECHNOLOGY, COORDINATED PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY AND LSL SERVICES, AND FAMILY SUPPORT. 4. EXPAND THE KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND CONNECTIONS AMONG FAMILIES OF CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING AND THE PROFESSIONALS WHO SUPPORT THEM. 5. EXERCISE NATIONAL LEADERSHIP AS AN EXEMPLARY FOUNDATION. WE KNOW THAT EARLY LEARNING IS CRITICAL TO A CHILD'S LANGUAGE DEVELOPMENT AND LIFETIME SUCCESS, AND CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING SHOULD HAVE THE SAME OPPORTUNITIES AS THEIR HEARING PEERS TO CAPITALIZE ON THE CRITICAL EARLY PERIOD FOR BRAIN DEVELOPMENT. OUR VISION FOR THE FUTURE IS THAT QUALITY PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY AND LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE INTERVENTION WILL BE AVAILABLE AND AFFORDABLE FOR ALL FAMILIES AS CLOSE AS POSSIBLE TO THEIR BABY'S BIRTH OR ACQUIRED HEARING LOSS, AND FAMILIES WILL BE EMPOWERED AND EQUIPPED TO EFFECTIVELY SUPPORT THEIR BABY'S LEARNING, SPOKEN LANGUAGE, AND LITERACY DEVELOPMENT. IN PURSUIT OF OUR MISSION AND VISION, THE OBERKOTTER FOUNDATION IS POISED TO CREATE TRANSFORMATIVE IMPACT BY DRIVING SCIENCE AND INNOVATION, SPEARHEADING GROUNDBREAKING IMPACT, INFLUENCING POLICY, AND FOSTERING COLLABORATIONS TO IMPROVE AUDIOLOGICAL OUTCOMES AND ENHANCE THE DEVELOPMENT OF LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE AND LITERACY SKILLS FOR CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING. HEARING FIRST: EVERY PARENT NEEDS TO KNOW THE STATUS OF THEIR BABY'S HEARING FIRST, BECAUSE THE EARLIER A CHILD WITH HEARING LOSS IS IDENTIFIED, AMPLIFIED, AND RECEIVING HELP, THE MORE OPPORTUNITIES THAT CHILD WILL HAVE. IN OCTOBER 2015, OBERKOTTER LAUNCHED ITS WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARY, HEARING FIRST (WWW.HEARINGFIRST.ORG) TO HELP CARING ADULTS SUPPORT INFANTS AND TODDLERS WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING TO HAVE THE SAME LISTENING, SPOKEN LANGUAGE, AND LITERACY OPPORTUNITIES AS THEIR HEARING FRIENDS. FOR CHILDREN WITH HEARING LOSS, THE FIRST DAYS, WEEKS, AND MONTHS OF LIFE ARE CRITICAL. HEARING FIRST SUPPORTS FAMILIES DURING THIS PERIOD AND THROUGHOUT THEIR JOURNEY OF RAISING THEIR CHILD. ENTIRELY ONLINE, HEARING FIRST IS AVAILABLE FOR FAMILIES WHEN THEY NEED INFORMATION, A RESOURCE, A CONNECTION WITH ANOTHER FAMILY OR SIMPLY SOMEONE TO TELL THEM WHAT TO DO NEXT. HEARING FIRST REACHES FAMILIES THROUGH THE HEARING FIRST COMMUNITY, ITS SOCIAL PLATFORMS, AND, IMPORTANTLY, THE PROFESSIONALS IN A FAMILY'S LIFE. HEARING FIRST EQUIPS PROFESSIONALS WITH THE RESOURCES, COMMUNITY, AND LEARNING EXPERIENCES THEY NEED TO IMPROVE OUTCOMES FOR CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING THROUGH LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE (LSL). FAMILY SUPPORT: HEARING FIRST EMPLOYS A VARIETY OF TOOLS, ACTIVITIES, AND RESOURCES TO SUPPORT FAMILIES OF CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING AND WHO ARE SEEKING A LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE OUTCOME. THE HEARING FIRST FAMILY SUPPORT COMMUNITY HAS OVER 2,131 CURRENT MEMBERS. IN 2023, HEARING FIRST STARTED SHARING VIDEOS ON YOUTUBE AND OTHER SOCIAL MEDIA RESOURCES TO ANSWER COMMON QUESTIONS PARENTS HAVE ABOUT NAVIGATING THE JOURNEY WITH THEIR CHILD WHO IS DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING. OUR FAMILY-DIRECTED RESOURCES AVAILABLE ON OUR WEBSITE HAVE BEEN DOWNLOADED OVER 461,598 TIMES. LEARNING EXPERIENCES: HEARING FIRST CONTINUES TO OFFER LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE (LSL) AND PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY LEARNING EXPERIENCES (LES) TO BOTH FAMILY AND PROFESSIONALS. IN 2024 WE PROVIDED NEW AND VARIED COURSES WITH HIGH-QUALITY, EVIDENCE-BASED EDUCATIONAL CONTENT TO ADVANCE THE LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE OUTCOMES OF INFANTS AND CHILDREN WITH HEARING LOSS. THESE LEARNING EXPERIENCES CONTINUE TO HIGHLIGHT THE ADVANCEMENTS AND INNOVATIONS IN THE PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY FIELD. HEARING FIRST IS AN APPROVED CONTINUING EDUCATION PROVIDER WITH THE AMERICAN SPEECH-LANGUAGE-HEARING ASSOCIATION (ASHA), AMERICAN ACADEMY OF AUDIOLOGY (AAA), AND THE AG BELL ACADEMY FOR LISTENING AND SPOKEN LANGUAGE AT NO COST TO LEARNERS. THERE HAVE BEEN OVER 53,782 COURSE REGISTRATIONS SINCE THE LAUNCH OF HEARING FIRST ONLINE LEARNING.SELF-STUDY LEARNING EXPERIENCES (LES): HEARING FIRST ADDED SELF-STUDY LES AS AN OPTION FOR PROFESSIONAL AND FAMILY LEARNING. THE SELF-STUDY LES ARE THE SAME HIGH-QUALITY, EVIDENCE-BASED EDUCATIONAL CONTENT AND ARE APPROVED FOR CONTINUING EDUCATION CREDITS FROM ASHA, AAA, AND AG BELL. THIS OPTION PROVIDES FLEXIBILITY FOR PROFESSIONAL AND FAMILY LEARNERS TO ENGAGE WITH THE COURSE MATERIAL AT THEIR OWN PACE, ACCOMMODATING DIFFERENT SCHEDULES AND LEARNING PREFERENCES. THE LEARNERS CAN REVISIT KEY CONCEPTS AS NEEDED WHILE STILL GAINING ACCESS TO THE SAME HIGH-QUALITY CONTENT OFFERED IN LIVE SESSIONS. THERE HAVE BEEN OVER 7,857 SELF-STUDY COURSE REGISTRATIONS SINCE THE LAUNCH OF HEARING FIRST SELF-STUDY LES. PROFESSIONAL LEARNING COMMUNITY: SINCE ITS LAUNCH IN OCTOBER 2016, THE PROFESSIONAL LEARNING COMMUNITY (PLC) HAS INCREASED TO 12,465 PROFESSIONAL MEMBERS WHO PROVIDE SERVICES TO CHILDREN WHO ARE DEAF OR HARD OF HEARING AND THEIR FAMILIES. THE PLC OFFERS A PROFESSIONAL HOME FOR LSL & PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY PROFESSIONALS TO SHARE IDEAS, DISCUSS IMPORTANT TOPICS, AND IMPROVE THEIR PRACTICE. PROFESSIONS REPRESENTED INCLUDE SPEECH-LANGUAGE PATHOLOGY, PEDIATRIC AUDIOLOGY, DEAF EDUCATION, EARLY INTERVENTION, EARLY CHILDHOOD EDUCATION, PSYCHOLOGY, BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, CHILDCARE, AND ANCILLARY HEALTHCARE PROVIDERS. STARTS HEAR AWARENESS CAMPAIGN: IN FEBRUARY 2024, HEARING FIRST BEGAN THE FOURTH YEAR OF THE STARTS HEAR AWARENESS CAMPAIGN (WWW.STARTSHEAR.ORG). THIS CAMPAIGN TARGETS EXPECTANT MOMS TO INFORM THEM ABOUT THE IMPORTANCE OF HEARING FOR BRAIN DEVELOPMENT TO RAISE AWARENESS OF THE NEWBORN HEARING SCREENING (NHS) THEIR CHILD WILL UNDERGO SHORTLY AFTER BIRTH, AND HOW TO FOLLOW UP IN THE EVENT THEIR BABY FAILS THE NHS SCREENING. THE STARTS HEAR AWARENESS CAMPAIGN IS CONDUCTED ENTIRELY ONLINE THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA AND PAID DIGITAL ADVERTISING CHANNELS TO REACH EXPECTANT MOMS WITH THE MESSAGE ABOUT THE IMPORTANCE OF HEARING HEALTH FOR A CHILD'S DEVELOPMENT. IN YEAR FOUR, THE STARTS HEAR CAMPAIGN EXTENDED THE REACH OF CAMPAIGN MESSAGES IN SPANISH ON BABYCENTER EN ESPANOL, A PARENTING SITE FOR NATIVE SPANISH SPEAKING FAMILIES. THE CAMPAIGN HAS BEEN ENDORSED BY SUCH ORGANIZATIONS AS: AMERICAN ACADEMY OF AUDIOLOGY, ZERO TO THREE, BRAZELTON TOUCHPOINTS CENTER, AND CENTER FOR EARLY LEARNING AND PUBLIC HEALTH. THROUGH CAMPAIGN CHANNELS AND PARTNERSHIPS INCLUDING BABYCENTER, WHAT TO EXPECT, AND THE BUMP, OUR MESSAGES HAVE GARNERED OVER 904 MILLION IMPRESSIONS WITH OUR AUDIENCE TO DATE. THE CAMPAIGN'S 15 SECOND VIDEOS HAVE BEEN VIEWED ALL THE WAY THROUGH NEARLY 113 MILLION TIMES.

ELECTION TO TREAT UNUSED PRIOR YEAR CORPUS DISTRIBUTIONS AS CURRENT YEAR CORPUS DISTRIBUTIONS:PURSUANT TO REG. 53.4942(A)-3(C)(2)(IV), THIS FOUNDATION ELECTS TO TREAT, AS A CURRENT CORPUS DISTRIBUTION, THE FOLLOWING UNUSED PRIOR TAX YEARS' DISTRIBUTIONS THAT WERE TREATED AS CORPUS DISTRIBUTIONS UNDER REG. 53.4942(A)-3(D)(1)(III) IN THESE PRIOR TAX YEAR(S):TAX YEAR: 2020 AMOUNT: $705,157SIGNED: TERESA CARAWAY, CEODATE: APRIL 28, 2025THIS RETURN IS BEING ELECTRONICALLY FILED, THE FORM 8879-TE IS BEING SIGNED BY AN INDIVIDUAL WHO IS ALSO AUTHORIZED TO SIGN THIS ELECTION, TERESA CARAWAY, CEO. THE SIGNATURE ON THE FORM 8879-TE IS DEEMED A SIGNATURE BY AN AUTHORIZED INDIVIDUAL FOR THIS ELECTION. A SIGNED COPY OF THIS ELECTION IS ALSO BEING KEPT ON FILE AS PART OF THE FOUNDATION'S TAX RECORDS.
